plan.title: Validate GitHub auth mode inside the actual worker environment

narratives.Description: The observed failure showed parent shell GitHub authentication can pass while the worker sandbox still cannot authenticate or reach GitHub. This story adds deterministic validation for host-gh and injected-token modes from the environment that will perform GitHub operations.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Add the scripts/github_auth_modes.py file that consumes runtime probe output and validates host-gh versus injected-token GitHub modes with gh auth and a minimal API reachability check.
2. Add pytest verification evidence in tests/cli/test_github_auth_modes.py for missing injected credentials, valid host-gh local mode, sandbox auth failure, and repo-access failure remediation.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm dispatcher, I want GitHub auth validation to run inside the worker environment, so that local workers fail before dispatch when host credentials are unusable.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1557 decomposition recommendation item 2; issue #1557 auth-mode validation AC.

narrative.Acceptance: Given github_auth_mode is injected-token, when no GH_TOKEN or GITHUB_TOKEN is available, then validation fails closed and does not fall back to host gh state.

items.title: Host-gh mode validates actual worker reachability

narrative.Acceptance: Given github_auth_mode is host-gh, when validation runs from the worker environment, then it checks gh auth status and a minimal GitHub API call from that same environment.

Given parent host auth works but worker sandbox GitHub auth fails, when validation reports the failure, then it names full-access execution, trusted command allowlisting, or injected-token handoff as remediation.

expected_outputs: GitHub auth-mode tests fail closed for missing injected token and worker-environment auth failures

references.title: Issue #1557: swarm: make GitHub credential policy runtime-aware for local agents
